source_meaning: paper lanterns to temple bells; there is no comparison as though look similar, they are completely different
target_meaning: the tranquil and desolate scenery in Buddhist temples

source_meaning: beyond imagination
target_meaning: remote place; faraway

source_meaning: The more the better.
target_meaning: the more the merrier

source_meaning: selfishness, self-centeredness
target_meaning: the quality of being egocentric as a hypothetical center of the universe

source_meaning: reliable, dependable, trustworthy

source_meaning: to get hungry
target_meaning: having a full belly can make one sleepy

source_meaning: grass roots
target_meaning: homegrown

source_meaning: as soon as
target_meaning: sooner or later

source_meaning: to each his own; different strokes for different folks; there's no accounting for taste
target_meaning: there is never such thing as consensus in a straight discussion; so many men, so many minds

source_meaning: to throw out one's chest; to be puffed up with pride
target_meaning: to be arrogant despite one's ignorance; to be egotistical

source_meaning: pinch pennies, live a stingy life
target_meaning: to save some money just in case one may need it.

source_meaning: being a piece of cake (easy)
target_meaning: very easy

source_meaning: tiny, not enough room to swing a cat
target_meaning: packed like sardines

source_meaning: sneak thief, literally "to steal like a dog"
target_meaning: a thug (a criminal who treats others violently or roughly)

source_meaning: to displease someone (due to one's speech or conduct), to be frowned upon
target_meaning: to be mediocre or displeasing

source_meaning: to take on a fight, to take up the gauntlet

target_meaning: a person who likes judging their surroundings out of ignorance

source_meaning: (idiomatic) to be jealous (usually of a rival in love)
target_meaning: to be bursting with jealousy; to be jealous; to hate on others because of jealousy

source_meaning: to hate the very sight of; to hold a grudge against; to treat like an enemy; to bear constant enmity; to be gunning for; to have it in for
target_meaning: to have an extreme and utter hatred

source_meaning: forcibly
target_meaning: to use force to coerce someone into submission is better than to use reason

source_meaning: to hide one's talents and conceal their existence
target_meaning: to put a lot of effort into hiding something bad

source_meaning: be intolerable
target_meaning: discordant and intolerable

source_meaning: unreliable, undependable, untrustworthy
target_meaning: treacherous

source_meaning: baseless, unsubstantiated, having no evidence

source_meaning: to avoid being seen; to be clandestine
target_meaning: any act carried out so clandestinely and secretly that all traces are erased, especially illicit affairs

source_meaning: deceive, play a trick on someone

target_meaning: to work outside (as opposed to indoor) all day long

target_idiom: ngậm máu phun người, trước dơ miệng mình
source_meaning: chickens come home to roost, so you should be prepared for your curse to come back to you. Also said to admonish someone against cursing another person casually.
target_meaning: Curses are like young chickens, they always come home to roost.

source_meaning: so busy there are not enough hours in the day; to want to borrow even the paw of a cat

source_meaning: Rome wasn't built in a day

target_meaning: each and every move; every single action

source_meaning: to be hostile

source_idiom: 下手な鉄砲も数撃てば当たる

source_meaning: the good and bad deeds cancel out
target_meaning: it is never too late to stop generating negative karma () and start seeking enlightenment instead

source_idiom: 病は口より入り、禍は口より出ず

source_meaning: with unstoppable force and vigor
target_meaning: lively and vigorous

source_meaning: completely soaked
target_meaning: completely empty

source_meaning: a word of great value

source_meaning: to steal someone's heart; to be thoroughly attracted by; to be completely fascinated by
target_meaning: to be sincerely convinced; to be won over completely

source_meaning: to win, to gain a victory
target_meaning: to get wins after wins; to rake victories

source_meaning: the course of events by Heaven, God's providence

source_meaning: all things existing in the universe
target_meaning: all-encompassing; totally inclusive; affecting all things

source_meaning: to take one's hat off; to acknowledge another's superiority

source_meaning: straight to the point; point-blank; frank; direct​
